title: dryopteris hirtula
intro: Dryopteris hirtula, also known as the hairy wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Dryopteris hirtula include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and promote wound healing. Dryopteris hirtula can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine as a component of various herbal formulas.
